setting.note: Conrad steamed up the Congo himself in 1890 and Marlow's journey retraces his own. The places are never named in the book, but they map onto a real route: from the Company's Outer Station at Matadi on the lower river, overland past the cataracts to the Central Station at Leopoldville (now Kinshasa), and on up the great bend to the Inner Station near Stanley Falls (now Kisangani). The bbox spans that whole run.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Joseph Conrad, first published 1899; the author died in 1924.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1924).
rights.attribution: Joseph Conrad, Heart of Darkness (1902). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: the heart of an immense darkness
palette.note: The grey-black of the gloom the whole book is drawn toward, taken from its closing words. Clears WCAG AA on paper.
